snakeATAC.utility 1.0.0

plot.BINDetect.volcano

Source: R/plot.BINDetect.volcano.R
plot.BINDetect.volcano.Rd

Function to replot the volcano plot of differential motifs (footprints) as generated by BINDetect.

# S3 method for class 'BINDetect.volcano'
plot(
  results,
  motif.pattern = "_(MOUSE|HUMAN)[.]H[0-9]*MO",
  right.color = "indianred",
  left.color = "steelblue",
  ns.color = "gray30",
  points.transparency = 0.5,
  labels.min.segment.length = 0,
  add.signif.lables = TRUE,
  labels.repel.force = 10,
  labels.max.overlaps = 100,
  extra.labels = "none"
)

Arguments

results

Path to the TOBIAS BINDetect results table, e.g. bindetect_results.txt, or a corresponding data.frame.

motif.pattern

String of the regular expression indicating the pattern to remove from the motif ID. Default: "_(MOUSE|HUMAN)[.]H[0-9]*MO".

right.color

String indicating the color to use for the differential motifs in the right side (positive difference) of the plot. Default: "indianred".

left.color

String indicating the color to use for the differential motifs in the left side (negative difference) of the plot. Default: "steelblue".

ns.color

String indicating the color to use for the non-significant motifs of the plot. Default: "gray30".

points.transparency

Numeric value between 0-1 indicating the transparency (alpha) of the dots in the volcano. Default: 0.5.

labels.min.segment.length

Numeric value indicating the minimum length of the line connecting the motif labels and the corresponding dot (passed to ggrepel). Default: 0, segment always present.

add.signif.lables

Logical value indicating whether differential motifs should be labelled. Default: TRUE.

labels.repel.force

Numeric value indicated the force of repulsion between overlapping text labels (passed to ggrepel). Default: 10.

labels.max.overlaps

Numeric value indicating of maximum labels overlapping allowed (passed to ggrepel). Default: 100.

extra.labels

Vector of other motifs to display, irrespective of their significance. Default: "none".

Value

A ggplot object.

Contents

Developed by Sebastian Gregoricchio.
